positive.prompt: Render my .ux/audits reports to HTML so I can open them in a browser
Make a shareable HTML page of the audit with the screenshot walkthrough as a flipbook
Turn the latest audit report into a self-contained HTML view

negative.prompt: Run a usability audit of the checkout flow
Verify my critical user journeys still pass against the running app

evals.prompt: In the Sprout repo, whose .ux/audits already holds usability, accessibility, web-performance, cuj, and rollup reports from a prior run, run /ux-agent-skills:ux-review to render them all to HTML.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained <report>.html beside every <report>.md under Sprout/.ux/audits, the roll-up rendered as a go/no-go dashboard, and an index.html landing page — each opening offline with images inlined, and no report Markdown modified.
expectations: Writes one <report>.html beside each existing <report>.md under Sprout/.ux/audits, plus index.html — and does NOT create, edit, or delete any .md report or the report contract
Every generated .html is self-contained: images are inlined as data: URIs and there are no external (http/https/protocol-relative) src or href references, so it opens offline from disk
The cuj report with total: 0 renders a 'verified pass' banner (never an empty 'no findings'), and its walkthrough renders as an interactive flipbook grouped by journey
The rollup-*.md renders as a dashboard with the go/no-go verdict and a per-auditor severity table whose report links point at the .html views
Re-running /ux-review on an unchanged report produces a byte-identical .html (deterministic — no diff churn)
All writes are confined to Sprout/.ux/audits/ — no host application code is touched, and audit_safety.py <sprout> reports no violation

evals.prompt: In a repo that has no .ux/audits directory yet, run /ux-agent-skills:ux-review.
evals.expected_output: It stops and says there are no reports to render, pointing at /usability-audit, /cuj-audit, or /ux-audit — and creates no files.
expectations: Does not fabricate a report or an empty HTML page; it stops because there is nothing to render
Names how to produce a report first (/usability-audit, /cuj-audit, or /ux-audit)
